messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Who wrote the Meditations, and could it have been written by Epictetus or Plato?

The Meditations (沉思錄) was written by Marcus Aurelius, the Roman Emperor who reigned from 161 to 180 CE. Scholars attribute this work to him with confidence — it is a private journal he kept in Greek, never intended for publication, reflecting his Stoic philosophical practice. It could not have been written by Epictetus or Plato. Epictetus, although a major Stoic influence on Marcus Aurelius, was a freed slave philosopher who lived in the 1st–2nd century CE; his own teachings were recorded by his student Arrian in works like the Discourses and the Enchiridion. Plato lived centuries earlier, in the 4th–5th century BCE, and was a founder of the entirely different Platonic tradition. The Meditations is solely the work of Marcus Aurelius.
